title: Add tcp-established to checkpoint/restore
body: This adds the possibility to checkpoint and restore containers with established TCP connections. See third commit for details and examples. This PR also includes updates to the bash completion which was missing a few of the recently added checkpoint/restore features.

comments.body: See https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cri-o/pull/1926 for required conmon changes.
FYI @rst0git
Since this depends on conmon changes, I'll hold off on adding an approved tag until that merges
Hmm, all checkpoint tests are failing, strange. It works locally. Need to figure what is going on.

Ah, so I have an idea why the checkpoint tests are failing. Since today criu 3.11 is part of all Fedora branches and now a criu version is automatically installed which has all the features for Podman.

Up until now the container in container checkpoint tests were not run, because the CRIU version in the container was too old. Now it is new enough, but CRIU cannot handle to checkpoint a container running in another container. Need to try the test cases (Podman in Podman) locally.
Pushed a test commit. See also https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1855

> Pushed a test commit. See also #1855

the patch LGTM.  If it fixes the CI failures it is probably worth to move it to its own PR so to unblock other PRs as well
the patch fixes the tests, could you please move it to a separate PR?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1858) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Since this PR is adding support for `tcp-established` to enable checkpoint of established TCP connections, should we also add support for [`skip-in-flight`](https://criu.org/CLI/opt/--skip-in-flight)?

> Since this PR is adding support for `tcp-established` to enable checkpoint of established TCP connections, should also add support for `skip-in-flight` and `tcp-close` for completeness?

Yes and no :grin: The reason to not add it is to have only 'one' change in this PR and the real reason is, that runc does not yet support those CRIU flags. So first the changes need to be added to runc and then we can look at adding those features to Podman. Which makes more sense as another PR.

@TomSweeneyRedHat thanks a lot for your review. I addressed all your reviews and force pushed a version.

Without the changes which are now part of #1858
necessary conmon changes have been merged
/restest
/approve

Let's see if the tests pick up the new conmon version or if we hardcode a commit somewhere
The CI failures seem to be related to problems with the container registries.
/retest
bot, retest this please
bot, retest this please

Cirrus timeout is suspicious - I re-triggered, but we'll see.

Really annoying it's not actually throwing whatever error caused it to hang, if it is one.

@adrianreber Seems to be timing out trying to restore?

`Running: /usr/bin/podman --root /tmp/podman_test449995664/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test449995664/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --storage-driver=vfs container restore -l`

@mheon thanks for looking into it. It seems my test for testing checkpointing with tcp-established does not work correctly in the CI. I am starting a container with --network=host and I am trying to connect to it. It works locally, but probably not in the CI. I will remove the test case to see if that helps. Too many changes in one PR it seems.

I also see errors like this in cirrus https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6388882125881344:
```
varlink.error.VarlinkError: {'parameters': {'reason': 'cgroups: cgroup mountpoint does not exist'}, 'error': 'io.podman.ErrorOccurred'}```

Is this a known problem? Or triggered by my changes?
Known flake that seems to have gotten a lot more common now - previously was 1 in 10 or 15 runs, now seems like it's happening every time. I'll grab @jwhonce and see if I can get a fix in.

Cool, everything green. This includes an updated conmon and new CRIU version. Instead of pointing to the CRIU development branch which will be rebased/rewritten occasionally this now points to the stable branch which has stable commits.

From my point of view this is no longer WIP as the necessary conmon changes have been merged and pulled in in this PR for testing.

LGTM
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 @jwhonce @vrothberg PTAL

Looks fine, merging
/lgtm
